raw_response:
I concur with the previous assessment. While deletions affecting chromosome 21 do occur and may cause similar symptoms due to loss of function rather than gain, these events are generally referred to separately under different syndromes such as Cri du Chat syndrome. These deletions do not fall within the typical definition used for diagnosing Down syndrome which involves overabundance of genetic material. Thus, option D remains appropriately identified as the exception here.

Answer Choice: D.
